tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 532 unit tests passed before the Redis-specific fixes.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone loaded successfully on http://localhost:3015.

Queried AWS with `aws elasticache describe-cache-clusters --cache-cluster-id namuh-linear-redis --region us-east-1 --show-cache-node-info`; AWS returned `CacheClusterNotFound`, confirming the app-specific ElastiCache cluster is not provisioned.

bugs_found.description: The clone does not have a provisioned or configured app-specific Redis backend for infra-002.
bugs_found.expected: A reachable `namuh-linear` Redis/ElastiCache instance should exist and `REDIS_URL` should be configured so the clone can use real caching and pub/sub.
bugs_found.actual: `.env` has no `REDIS_URL`, the shell environment has no `REDIS_URL`, and AWS reports `CacheClusterNotFound` for `namuh-linear-redis`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Run `env | rg '^REDIS_URL='` and inspect `.env` for `REDIS_URL`.
2. Run `aws elasticache describe-cache-clusters --cache-cluster-id namuh-linear-redis --region us-east-1 --show-cache-node-info`.
3. Observe the missing env var and the AWS `CacheClusterNotFound` response.

fix_description: Fixed two Redis helper bugs discovered during QA: replaced wildcard invalidation based on blocking `KEYS` with cursor-based `SCAN`, and deduplicated pub/sub message listeners so repeated subscriptions do not leak handlers. `qa_pass` remains false because real `namuh-linear` ElastiCache provisioning/configuration is still missing.

Provisioned the missing Redis infrastructure in AWS for this clone: created `namuh-linear-app-sg`, created `namuh-linear-redis-sg` with port 6379 ingress from the app security group, created the `namuh-linear-redis-subnet` cache subnet group, created the `namuh-linear-redis` ElastiCache Redis cluster, and waited for it to reach `available`.

Verified the post-fix state in AWS: `namuh-linear-redis` is `available` in `us-east-1`, backed by subnet group `namuh-linear-redis-subnet`, attached to security group `sg-0dddf0e0b46c8cffa`, and exposes endpoint `namuh-linear-redis.szjnmv.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379`.
Confirmed the local environment is now configured for the provisioned backend by appending `REDIS_URL=redis://namuh-linear-redis.szjnmv.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379` to `.env`.

bugs_found.description: The clone still lacked a provisioned and configured app-specific ElastiCache Redis backend at the start of attempt 3.
bugs_found.expected: A reachable `namuh-linear` Redis/ElastiCache instance should exist in AWS and the clone should have `REDIS_URL` configured to that endpoint.
bugs_found.actual: No `REDIS_URL` was configured locally and AWS returned `CacheClusterNotFound` for `namuh-linear-redis` before the fix.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 533 unit tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.
Opened the clone with Ever, captured a login-page snapshot, and saved a screenshot to `ralph/screenshots/qa/infra-003-attempt-1-login.jpg` to confirm the app was reachable during infrastructure QA.

Verified the pre-fix failure state: the local environment had no `S3_BUCKET` configured, `aws s3api head-bucket --bucket namuh-linear-assets-us-east-1 --region us-east-1` returned `404`, and `aws s3api get-bucket-cors --bucket namuh-linear-assets-us-east-1 --region us-east-1` returned `NoSuchBucket`.
Provisioned the missing S3 bucket in AWS and applied the expected CORS policy allowing `GET`, `PUT`, and `POST` from any origin with exposed `ETag`.
Configured the local runtime with `AWS_REGION=us-east-1` and `S3_BUCKET=namuh-linear-assets-us-east-1` in `.env`.

Ran a live Node-based S3 probe through `src/lib/s3.ts`: `uploadFile` uploaded an avatar object, `getUploadUrl` generated a presigned `PUT` URL for an attachment object, both `getDownloadUrl` fetches returned `200`, and the downloaded bodies matched the uploaded content before cleanup.

bugs_found.description: The clone was missing the provisioned S3 bucket and local runtime configuration required for real avatar and attachment storage.
bugs_found.expected: A reachable `namuh-linear` S3 bucket should exist in AWS with the expected CORS policy, and the clone should have `S3_BUCKET` and `AWS_REGION` configured so `src/lib/s3.ts` can generate working presigned URLs and uploads.
bugs_found.actual: The expected bucket did not exist in AWS, `head-bucket` returned `404`, `get-bucket-cors` returned `NoSuchBucket`, and the local `.env` lacked `S3_BUCKET` and `AWS_REGION`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Run `aws s3api head-bucket --bucket namuh-linear-assets-us-east-1 --region us-east-1`.
2. Run `aws s3api get-bucket-cors --bucket namuh-linear-assets-us-east-1 --region us-east-1`.
3. Inspect `.env` for missing `S3_BUCKET` and `AWS_REGION`.
4. Observe that real S3-backed upload/download cannot work until the bucket is provisioned and configured.

fix_description: Provisioned the missing S3 bucket in AWS, applied the required CORS policy, configured the local runtime with `AWS_REGION` and `S3_BUCKET`, and verified the real helper with successful live avatar upload plus presigned attachment upload/download.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 533 unit tests passed before the email fix.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.

Verified AWS SES account state in `us-east-1`: `ProductionAccessEnabled=true`, `SendingEnabled=true`, and verified sending domain `foreverbrowsing.com` via `aws sesv2 get-account`, `list-email-identities`, and `get-email-identity`.

Reproduced the real failure by invoking `src/lib/email.ts` with the current runtime env; `sendMagicLinkEmail` to `success@simulator.amazonses.com` failed with `MessageRejected` because the clone fell back to unverified sender `noreply@namuh-linear.example.com`.
Updated the fallback sender to the verified SES domain and added a regression test covering the no-`SENDER_EMAIL` path.

Verified real SES acceptance through the actual app helper functions: `sendMagicLinkEmail`, `sendInvitationEmail`, and `sendNotificationEmail` all succeeded when sending to `success@simulator.amazonses.com`.

Verified the user-facing magic-link flow in the browser: `/login` -> `Continue with Email` -> submit `success@simulator.amazonses.com` -> `Check your email` confirmation rendered successfully. Saved screenshot to `ralph/screenshots/qa/infra-004-attempt-1-login-magic-link.png`.

Ran `make check` and `make test` after the fix; both passed. Also ran `make test-e2e` as a cross-feature check: smoke passed, while unrelated command-palette specs still failed outside this feature area.

bugs_found.description: The clone's email utility defaulted to an unverified SES sender address, so magic-link auth emails and other application emails failed in the real environment when `SENDER_EMAIL` was unset.
bugs_found.expected: The clone should send mail from a verified SES identity so magic links, invitations, and notifications succeed even when the local runtime does not explicitly set `SENDER_EMAIL`.
bugs_found.actual: The helper fell back to `noreply@namuh-linear.example.com`, and SES rejected live sends with `MessageRejected: Email address is not verified`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Ensure `.env` has no `SENDER_EMAIL` entry and source it into the shell.
2. Run `npx tsx --eval 'import { sendMagicLinkEmail } from "./src/lib/email.ts"; (async () => { await sendMagicLinkEmail("success@simulator.amazonses.com", "123456", "http://localhost:3015/api/auth/verify?token=test"); })().catch((error) => { console.error(error); process.exit(1); });'`.
3. Observe SES rejecting the send because `noreply@namuh-linear.example.com` is not a verified identity in `us-east-1`.

fix_description: Changed the email helper fallback sender from the dead `.example.com` address to `noreply@foreverbrowsing.com`, which is covered by the verified `foreverbrowsing.com` SES identity, and added a regression test for the unset-`SENDER_EMAIL` path.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 534 unit tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.

Opened `/login` in Ever, confirmed the login screen renders with the Google and email CTAs, and saved screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-001-attempt-1-login.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-001-attempt-1-after-google-click.jpg`.
Reproduced the initial Google OAuth failure: clicking `Continue with Google` did not leave `/login`, and `POST /api/auth/sign-in/social` returned `500`.

Inspected the live Next dev log and traced the `500` to Better Auth failing to open Postgres with `SASL: SCRAM-SERVER-FIRST-MESSAGE: client password must be a string` because `.env` had no `DATABASE_URL`.

Configured the app with a real local Postgres connection (`localhost:5432/namuh_linear`), pushed the Drizzle schema, and verified `user`, `account`, `session`, and `verification` tables now exist in Postgres.
Re-tested `POST /api/auth/sign-in/social` and confirmed it now returns `200` with a Google authorization URL and Better Auth state cookie.

Re-tested the browser flow in Ever and reached Google, but the provider rejects the request with `Error 400: redirect_uri_mismatch`; screenshot saved to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-001-attempt-1-google-oauth-page.jpg`.
Verified no auth records were created because the Google flow never reaches the callback: `/api/auth/get-session` returns `null`, and Postgres counts remain `user=0`, `account=0`, `session=0`.

bugs_found.description: The app runtime was missing `DATABASE_URL`, so Better Auth could not reach Postgres and Google sign-in crashed before generating an authorization redirect.
bugs_found.expected: The Google auth endpoint should open the Postgres-backed Better Auth tables and return a valid Google authorization URL when `Continue with Google` is clicked.
bugs_found.actual: `.env` had no `DATABASE_URL`, `POST /api/auth/sign-in/social` returned `500`, and the Next dev log showed `SASL: SCRAM-SERVER-FIRST-MESSAGE: client password must be a string`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Start the app with the pre-fix `.env`.
2. Open `/login` and click `Continue with Google`, or `curl -X POST http://localhost:3015/api/auth/sign-in/social -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Origin: http://localhost:3015' -d '{"provider":"google","callbackURL":"/"}'`.
3. Observe the request fail with `500` and the server log report the Postgres SCRAM password error.

bugs_found.description: Google OAuth is still blocked because the configured Google OAuth client does not authorize the clone's redirect URI.
bugs_found.expected: After clicking `Continue with Google`, Google should accept the auth request, redirect back to `/api/auth/callback/google`, create/link the user account on first login, and establish a session.
bugs_found.actual: Google renders `Access blocked: This app’s request is invalid` with `Error 400: redirect_uri_mismatch` for the clone's callback URI, so the flow never reaches the Better Auth callback and no user/account/session rows are created.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `http://localhost:3015/login`.
2. Click `Continue with Google`.
3. Observe the Google error page showing `Error 400: redirect_uri_mismatch` instead of completing the OAuth flow.

fix_description: Fixed the missing Postgres runtime configuration by restoring a real local `DATABASE_URL`, adding a development fallback for missing `DATABASE_URL` in the runtime and Drizzle config, creating/pushing the auth schema into Postgres, and restoring Better Auth's Google authorization redirect. `qa_pass` remains false because the Google OAuth client itself is externally misconfigured and still rejects the callback URI.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 536 unit tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.
Verified unauthenticated protected-route behavior with `curl http://localhost:3015/team/ABC/all`; the app returned `307` to `/login?callbackUrl=%2Fteam%2FABC%2Fall`.

Opened `http://localhost:3015/login` in Ever, confirmed the login screen rendered with the Google CTA, and saved screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-001-attempt-2-login.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-001-attempt-2-after-google.jpg`.
Verified `POST /api/auth/sign-in/social` returned `200` plus a Google authorization URL whose redirect target is `http://localhost:3015/api/auth/callback/google`.

Completed the real Google OAuth flow in Ever by selecting an existing Google account; the clone returned to `/create-workspace` for first-login onboarding and saved a screenshot to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-001-attempt-2-post-account.jpg`.

Verified the in-browser Better Auth session after Google callback via `fetch('/api/auth/get-session', { credentials: 'include' })`; it returned a real session plus user payload for `jaeyunha0317@gmail.com`.

Queried Postgres through the real `namuh_linear` database and confirmed a Google-linked `user` row, `account` row with `provider_id = 'google'`, and active `session` rows exist for the signed-in user.
Verified session persistence by navigating the authenticated browser to `/` and `/team/ABC/all`; both resolved to the first-login `/create-workspace` path instead of redirecting to `/login`.

Verified logout by posting to `/api/auth/sign-out` with JSON content; the response returned `200 {"success":true}`, `/api/auth/get-session` returned `null`, and navigating back to `/team/ABC/all` redirected to the login page again.
fix_description: No fix needed in attempt 2. Re-verified the previously repaired Better Auth/Postgres setup and confirmed Google OAuth, account creation, session creation, persistence, and logout now work end-to-end.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 541 unit tests passed after the auth fixes.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone still loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.

Posted directly to `POST /api/auth/sign-in/magic-link` and confirmed Better Auth now returns `200 {"status":true}` instead of a `500`.
Queried Postgres and confirmed magic-link verification rows now store real 6-digit numeric identifiers for the requested email address.

Ran a browser-level Playwright probe for a fresh simulator email address and verified the UI transitions to the code-entry screen; screenshot saved to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-002-attempt-1-code-entry.png`.

Completed the full browser flow by entering the stored 6-digit code, verified redirect to `/create-workspace`, confirmed `/api/auth/get-session` returned a live Better Auth session plus a newly created verified user, and saved the post-login state to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-002-attempt-1-post-login.png`.
Reloaded the authenticated page and confirmed the session persisted across refresh.

Verified sign-out with `POST /api/auth/sign-out` using the session cookie, confirmed `{"success":true}` plus cleared cookies, and confirmed a logged-out request to `/team/ABC/all` redirects to `/login?callbackUrl=%2Fteam%2FABC%2Fall`.
Verified the invalid-code edge case in a browser probe: entering `000000` redirects back to `/login?error=INVALID_TOKEN` and the page displays the mapped error message.
fix_description: Implemented real 6-digit Better Auth magic-link tokens, added the missing code-entry UI and invalid-code handling on `/login`, and added a non-production SES preview fallback so local magic-link auth keeps working when AWS credentials are unavailable.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 541 unit tests passed before the auth-003 fixes.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the clone loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.
Reproduced the main auth leak with live HTTP checks: unauthenticated `GET /create-workspace` and `GET /onboarding/invite?workspaceId=test` both returned `200 OK` instead of redirecting to login.
Captured the pre-fix unauthenticated workspace-creation screen in Ever and saved it to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-attempt-1-create-workspace-public.jpg`.
Verified the intended protected-route baseline still existed for app routes: unauthenticated `GET /team/ABC/all` returned `307` to `/login?callbackUrl=%2Fteam%2FABC%2Fall`.
Verified unauthenticated API behavior separately: `POST /api/workspaces` without a session returned `401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}`.
Fixed the route leak by removing `/create-workspace` and `/onboarding/*` from the middleware allowlist, then added regression coverage for both routes in `tests/middleware.test.ts`.

Fixed the login redirect bug by preserving the `callbackUrl` query parameter through Google sign-in, magic-link requests, and magic-link verification, with new regression coverage in `tests/login-page.test.tsx`.

Re-verified the logged-out browser flow in Ever after the fix: navigating to `/create-workspace` now shows the login page (`ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-attempt-1-create-workspace-logged-out.jpg`), and navigating to `/team/ABC/all` shows `/login?callbackUrl=%2Fteam%2FABC%2Fall` (`ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-attempt-1-team-redirect-login.jpg`).

Verified real Better Auth session creation with a fresh magic-link login via `POST /api/auth/sign-in/magic-link` plus `GET /api/auth/magic-link/verify`: `/api/auth/get-session` returned a live session for `auth003+1775541702@example.com`, and the session token existed in the Postgres `session` table.

Verified authenticated protected-route behavior with that real session: `GET /create-workspace` returned `200 OK`, and `GET /team/ABC/all` redirected to `/create-workspace` because the user was authenticated but had not created a workspace yet.

Verified logout cleanup end to end: `POST /api/auth/sign-out` returned `200 {"success":true}`, subsequent `/api/auth/get-session` requests returned `null`, and the previously issued session token no longer existed in the Postgres `session` table.
Ran `make check`; typecheck and Biome both passed.
Ran `make test`; all 546 unit tests passed after the auth-003 fixes.

Ran full `make test-e2e` because the fix touched shared auth middleware and login routing. Smoke passed, but the pre-existing unrelated command-palette specs still fail because they start from protected `/team/ENG/all` routes while logged out and land on the login page.

bugs_found.description: Unauthenticated users could open protected onboarding pages directly.
bugs_found.expected: Requests to `/create-workspace` and `/onboarding/*` should redirect unauthenticated users to `/login` with a `callbackUrl` so onboarding is only reachable behind a real Better Auth session.
bugs_found.actual: The middleware treated `/create-workspace` and `/onboarding` as public paths, so unauthenticated requests returned `200 OK` and rendered the onboarding UI.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Start the app without a Better Auth session.
2. Run `curl -i http://localhost:3015/create-workspace` and `curl -i 'http://localhost:3015/onboarding/invite?workspaceId=test'`.
3. Observe `200 OK` responses and the onboarding pages rendering instead of redirecting to `/login`.

bugs_found.description: The login page ignored `callbackUrl` when users were redirected from protected routes.
bugs_found.expected: When `/login` is opened with `?callbackUrl=...`, Google sign-in and magic-link verification should preserve that callback so the user returns to the originally requested protected route after authentication.
bugs_found.actual: The login client hardcoded `callbackURL: "/"` and `errorCallbackURL: "/login"`, which dropped the protected-route destination during both Google and magic-link sign-in.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/login?callbackUrl=%2Fteam%2FABC%2Fboard`.
2. Trigger `Continue with Google` or `Continue with Email`.
3. Inspect the sign-in request parameters or the magic-link verification URL and observe that they use `callbackURL=/` instead of the incoming `callbackUrl`.

fix_description: Removed protected onboarding pages from the middleware public allowlist, preserved `callbackUrl` throughout the login client flow, and added regression tests for both fixes. `qa_pass` is now true for auth-003; the remaining `make test-e2e` failures are pre-existing command-palette specs unrelated to this feature.

tested_steps: Ran `make check`; typecheck and Biome both passed after the auth-004 fixes.
Ran `make test`; all 548 unit tests passed.

Ran full `make test-e2e` because the fix touched shared auth routing/layout. Smoke passed, and the only failing specs were the pre-existing unrelated command-palette tests that still fail while logged out.
Opened the clone auth surface in Ever and confirmed `/login` renders the auth UI rather than the authenticated shell.
Compared the clone against fresh Playwright captures of `https://linear.app/login` and `https://linear.app/signup`; Linear exposes both dark/light auth surfaces and a shared login/signup entry point.
Reproduced the pre-fix auth-004 bugs: the clone stayed dark even under a light color-scheme and `/signup` had no implemented page.
Patched the auth UI to use a shared `/login` + `/signup` screen, updated the Linear-style icon/button treatment, and added light-theme auth tokens plus signup/login footer affordances.

Captured post-fix screenshots for the clone at `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-verified-login-dark.png`, `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-verified-login-light.png`, `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-verified-login-mobile.png`, `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-verified-signup-dark.png`, and `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-verified-signup-light.png`.

Saved the reference captures used for comparison at `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-linear-dark-desktop.png`, `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-linear-light-desktop.png`, and `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-004-attempt-1-linear-signup.png`.
Verified the browser email entrypoint still works by opening `Continue with Email` and confirming the email input renders on both `/login` and `/signup`.

Verified the Google entry point still returns a real OAuth redirect by posting to `POST /api/auth/sign-in/social`; the API returned `200` plus a Google authorization URL targeting `http://localhost:3015/api/auth/callback/google`.

bugs_found.description: The login/signup UI ignored light color-scheme preferences and stayed dark-only.
bugs_found.expected: The auth entry point should match Linear's light and dark presentations instead of forcing a dark-only screen.
bugs_found.actual: Fresh browser contexts with `prefers-color-scheme: light` still rendered the clone's login page on a black background with dark-only button styles.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `http://localhost:3015/login` in a browser context with `prefers-color-scheme: light`.
2. Observe the page stays dark instead of switching to a light auth surface.
3. Compare it with `https://linear.app/login`, which renders a light version under the same preference.

bugs_found.description: The clone did not implement `/signup`, so the auth UI did not actually handle both login and signup.
bugs_found.expected: The auth surface should support both `/login` and `/signup`, with the signup route presenting the same provider choices and the appropriate signup copy.
bugs_found.actual: There was no `src/app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx`, so `/signup` was missing even though the feature spec requires a shared login/signup entry point.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Inspect the auth routes before the fix and note that only `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` exists.

Exercised real notification creation end to end by creating issue `QAX2-39`, assigning it to the signed-in user, changing its workflow state, and posting a self-mention comment; `/inbox` then showed fresh `assigned`, `status_change`, and `mentioned` rows at the top of the list.

Captured QA evidence for the inbox page to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-020-attempt-1-inbox.png` and compared the overall split-pane structure against `ralph/screenshots/inspect/inbox.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/inspect/inbox-detail.jpg`.
fix_description: No feature-020 product bug required fixing. I repaired the stale Playwright auth setup so the required smoke coverage can authenticate reliably again, then verified the inbox feature end to end with real notifications and live API writes.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 620 unit tests passed before the feature-021 fixes.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; smoke passed on `http://localhost:3015` with the existing auth setup.

Logged into the clone via real Google OAuth with `TEST_ACCOUNT_EMAIL=jaeyunha0317@gmail.com`, opened `/my-issues/assigned`, and reproduced the initial failure: the page showed the empty state even though the active workspace already had assigned `QAX2-*` issues in Postgres.

Confirmed the root cause live by calling `/api/my-issues?tab=assigned` in the authenticated browser and observing `totalCount: 0`, then querying Postgres and verifying the signed-in user had assigned issues in the shell's active workspace.

Fixed the My Issues API to use the most recent workspace membership like the app shell, return project names for issue rows, merge subscribed/activity data from assignment, creation, and comments, and group workspace-wide states consistently across teams.

Re-verified `/my-issues/assigned` in Ever after the fix: the page now renders 18 real issues, including the pre-existing `QAX2-*` records plus a seeded cross-team `MIX-1` record, with status icons, identifiers, labels, assignee avatars, project breadcrumb text, and dates.
Opened the display-options menu and verified the `Project` property toggle hides and restores project breadcrumb text on the live list without affecting counts.

Applied a live `Status is Backlog` filter and verified the page reduced to the backlog rows with `16 hidden` in the header/footer, matching the 18 total assigned issues minus the 2 visible backlog issues.

Seeded a second team plus one assigned/commented issue in the same workspace, then verified cross-team rows render in the same My Issues view with distinct identifiers and without duplicate status sections.

Verified the `Created`, `Subscribed`, and `Activity` tabs all return live data for the active workspace; `Subscribed` now includes assignment/creation/comment-derived issues, and `Activity`/`Created` surface the newest `MIX-1` and `QAX2-39` rows first by date.
Verified `http://localhost:3015/my-issues` redirects to `/my-issues/assigned` in the authenticated browser.

Captured QA evidence screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-021-assigned-attempt-1.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-021-filtered-attempt-1.jpg`, and compared the overall structure against `ralph/screenshots/inspect/my-issues.jpg` / `my-issues-assigned.jpg`.
Ran `make check`; typecheck and Biome lint/format passed after formatting the touched route and generated JSON artifacts.
Ran `make test`; all 624 unit tests passed after adding My Issues regressions.

Ran full Playwright coverage with `make test-e2e` because the fix touched the shared `IssueRow` component. Smoke still passed, while the only failing specs were the pre-existing unrelated `tests/e2e/command-palette.spec.ts` cases where the command palette dialog never opens.

bugs_found.description: The My Issues API resolved a different workspace than the shell, so the page queried the wrong workspace for users with multiple memberships.
bugs_found.expected: My Issues should show the active workspace's assigned/created/subscribed/activity issues under the workspace currently rendered in the app shell.
bugs_found.actual: The shell showed `QA Fix 20260407 1644`, but `/api/my-issues?tab=assigned` returned `totalCount: 0` and the page rendered `No issues assigned` because the API selected an older workspace membership.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in with an account that belongs to multiple workspaces.
2. Open `http://localhost:3015/my-issues/assigned` in the newest workspace shown by the shell.
3. Observe the empty state even though the same user has assigned issues in that workspace.

bugs_found.description: Issue rows on My Issues omitted the PRD-required project breadcrumb.
bugs_found.expected: Rows with linked projects should display project breadcrumb text alongside the identifier/title metadata.
bugs_found.actual: The API did not return project names for My Issues rows, so project-linked issues rendered without any project breadcrumb.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `http://localhost:3015/my-issues/assigned` with project-linked issues in the active workspace.
2. Inspect rows like `QAX2-27` before the fix.
3. Observe the row shows identifier/title/date but no project name.

bugs_found.description: The Subscribed and Activity tabs were under-populated because they only approximated subscription/activity data.
bugs_found.expected: Subscribed should include issues surfaced by assignment, creation, and comment-based activity, and Activity should reflect recent issue interaction in the active workspace.
bugs_found.actual: Subscribed previously only looked at comment-linked issues, and Activity only merged assigned plus created issues without the commenter path.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Create or assign issues to the signed-in user without adding a comment.
2. Open `/my-issues/subscribed` and `/my-issues/activity` before the fix.
3. Observe the tabs miss issues that should appear from assignment/creation context.

bugs_found.description: Cross-team status filtering was broken because the filter menu duplicated the same status once per team and filtered only one team's state IDs.
bugs_found.expected: Workspace-wide My Issues status filters should show one entry per visible status group and filter matching issues across every team in the workspace.
bugs_found.actual: After adding a second team, the filter menu showed duplicate `Backlog`, `Todo`, `In Progress`, and `Done` entries, and selecting one only filtered a single team's state records.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Ensure the active workspace has at least two teams with default workflow states.
2. Open `http://localhost:3015/my-issues/assigned`.
3. Click `Add filter` then `Status`.
4. Observe duplicate status entries before the fix, and selecting one only matches one team's rows.

fix_description: Aligned the My Issues API with the shell's workspace selection, added project breadcrumb data to issue rows, broadened subscribed/activity aggregation to include assignment/creation/comment-derived issues, merged identical states across teams for workspace-wide views, and fixed status filtering so cross-team My Issues rows filter against shared grouped status options.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 624 then 630 unit tests passed while locking down the cycles and team-settings regressions.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; smoke passed with the Playwright auth fixture and the app loaded successfully on `http://localhost:3015`.

Ran full Playwright coverage with `npx playwright test`; the cycles/auth smoke paths still passed, while the only failing specs were the pre-existing unrelated `tests/e2e/command-palette.spec.ts` cases where the command palette dialog never opens.

Opened `/settings/teams/ONB/general` in Ever and reproduced the initial blocker where the page rendered `Team not found` because the backing `/api/teams/[key]/settings` route did not exist, then re-verified the fixed page loads real team data and persists cycle settings.

Verified the create-cycle form now uses local/team cadence defaults instead of the broken UTC-shifted date math: after enabling cycles for `ONB`, setting the start day to Tuesday and duration to 3 weeks, the form defaulted to `2026-04-14` through `2026-05-04` instead of the previous off-by-one UTC values.

Verified real API creation, overlap rejection, update, and deletion flows through the authenticated browser session: `POST /api/teams/ONB/cycles` created past/current/upcoming cycles, an overlapping create returned `409 {"error":"Cycle dates overlap with an existing cycle"}`, `PATCH /api/teams/ONB/cycles/[cycleId]` renamed the upcoming cycle, and `DELETE /api/teams/ONB/cycles/[cycleId]` removed a temporary cycle and unlinked issue `ONB-6` in Postgres.

Seeded real ONB issues into `QA Cycle Current` in Postgres and verified `/team/ONB/cycles/<cycleId>` groups them under Triage, Backlog, In Progress, and Done, with the progress summary reflecting the live issue distribution.

Verified the date-edge auto-rollover behavior in the browser after the timezone fix: with the team timezone still set to `America/Los_Angeles`, a cycle ending on `2026-04-07` correctly remained in `Current Cycle` while the cycle starting `2026-04-08` remained `Upcoming` on April 8, 2026 KST.

Captured QA evidence screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-030-cycles-list.png` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-030-cycle-detail.png`, and compared the overall structure against `ralph/screenshots/inspect/cycles.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/inspect/cycles-detail.jpg`.
Ran `make check` and `make test` after the fixes; both passed (`TypeCheck passed`, `Lint & Format passed`, `630` unit tests passed).

bugs_found.description: The team settings page for cycle configuration had no backing API route, so `/settings/teams/{key}/general` rendered `Team not found` and users could not configure cycle cadence at all.
bugs_found.expected: Team settings should load the active team's data and allow configuring cycle enablement, start day, and duration from the general settings page.
bugs_found.actual: The page fetched `/api/teams/{key}/settings`, but that route did not exist, so the browser rendered `Team not found` and there was no persistence path for cycle settings.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `http://localhost:3015/settings/teams/ONB/general` while signed in.
2. Observe the page render `Team not found` instead of the team settings form.
3. Confirm `src/app/api/teams/[key]/settings/route.ts` was missing before the fix.

bugs_found.description: Cycle creation and section categorization used UTC-based timestamp logic, producing off-by-one defaults and incorrect current/upcoming/past buckets around timezone boundaries.
bugs_found.expected: Cycle defaults should respect the configured team cadence, and cycle sections should be determined by calendar dates in the team's timezone so cycles remain current through their end date.
bugs_found.actual: The create form defaulted to the previous UTC date in KST, and the list categorized cycles with exact UTC timestamps, so a cycle ending on the team's current day could already show as past.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. On April 8, 2026 KST, open `/team/ONB/cycles` before the fix and start the create form.
2. Observe the start date default to `2026-04-07` because the page used `toISOString()`.
3. Create cycles ending `2026-04-07` and starting `2026-04-08`, keep the team timezone on `America/Los_Angeles`, and observe the list misclassify the end-date cycle as past instead of current.

bugs_found.description: The cycles API accepted invalid overlapping ranges and the UI hid the resulting backend validation failures.
bugs_found.expected: Cycle create/update should reject overlapping or malformed date ranges and surface the error to the user.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix there was no date-range validation, no overlap detection, and the form failed silently on non-OK responses.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Create one cycle for `ONB` covering `2026-04-08` to `2026-04-28`.
2. Attempt to create another covering `2026-04-20` to `2026-05-05`.
3. Before the fix, the API accepted overlapping ranges and the UI provided no useful error feedback.

fix_description: Added the missing team settings API, wired real cycle cadence controls and saving into team general settings, fixed local/team-date handling for cycle defaults and section categorization, validated cycle date formats/ranges/overlaps in the API, surfaced create errors in the UI, and re-verified create/update/delete/detail flows plus issue unlinking against the real database.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 633 unit tests passed after the initiative fixes.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; Playwright auth setup plus smoke both passed on `http://localhost:3015`.
Ran `make db-push`; the updated initiative schema, including persisted initiative settings for status updates, was applied to the local Postgres database.

Verified the initiatives page against real workspace data in Ever while signed in with Google: the empty-state copy rendered for `QA Fix 20260407 1644`, the `N then I` shortcut opened the create form, and real initiative creation populated the Active, Planned, and Completed tabs with correctly filtered records.

Verified the workspace-scoping fix end to end: after navigating from `/team/QAX2/all` into `/initiatives`, real initiative creates landed in the `QA Fix 20260407 1644` workspace instead of the previously wrong `Onboarding QA Team` workspace.

Verified initiative detail behavior on a real initiative: linked `Issues List QA Project` and `QA Progress 100 Project`, confirmed project progress rolled up from real issue counts (including a 100% completed linked project), changed the initiative status to `Completed`, and confirmed the UI and API persisted the transition.
Verified status updates with the real API by posting an `At risk` update and reloading the detail page; the update rendered with the health badge, author, timestamp, and body text.

Verified unlinking and deletion against the real database: removed one linked project from the initiative, then deleted the initiative entirely and confirmed the remaining `initiative_project` rows for that initiative dropped to `0` while the other QA initiatives remained.

Saved QA evidence screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-031-attempt-1-completed-list.png` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-031-attempt-1-planned-list.png`, and compared the workspace-level initiatives layout against `ralph/screenshots/inspect/initiatives.jpg`.

bugs_found.description: The initiatives APIs ignored the active workspace context, so list/create/detail requests could read and write initiatives in the wrong workspace.
bugs_found.expected: When the UI shows a workspace such as `QA Fix 20260407 1644`, initiative CRUD should operate in that same workspace.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, creating an initiative from the `QA Fix 20260407 1644` shell wrote the record into `Onboarding QA Team` because the routes just grabbed the first membership instead of the active workspace.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in as a user who belongs to multiple workspaces.
2. Navigate to `/team/QAX2/all` so the shell shows `QA Fix 20260407 1644`, then open `/initiatives`.
3. Create an initiative before the fix.
4. Query Postgres for the new initiative's `workspace_id` and observe that it belongs to a different workspace.

bugs_found.description: The initiative detail page did not provide the required management flows for project linking, status updates, or deletion.
bugs_found.expected: Initiative detail should let users link and unlink projects, post initiative updates, change status, and delete an initiative while keeping the list/detail views in sync.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, detail was effectively read-only aside from a bare API route; there were no visible controls for linking projects, posting updates, or deleting the initiative.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open any initiative detail page before the fix.
2. Look for controls to link a project, remove a project, post an update, or delete the initiative.
3. Observe that those flows are missing from the UI.

bugs_found.description: Linked project navigation was broken because initiative detail linked projects by raw project id instead of the slug-based project route.
bugs_found.expected: Clicking a linked project from initiative detail should open `/project/{slug}` for that project.
bugs_found.actual: The row href used `/project/{id}`, which does not match the app's project detail route and led to broken navigation.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Link a project to an initiative before the fix.
2. Inspect the linked project anchor on the initiative detail page.
3. Observe that it points to `/project/<project-id>` instead of `/project/<project-slug>`.

bugs_found.description: The `N then I` initiative shortcut was not implemented.
bugs_found.expected: Pressing `N` then `I` on the initiatives page should open the create initiative form.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the shortcut did nothing and users had to rely on the button only.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/initiatives`.
2. Press `N`, then `I`.
3. Observe that no create form opens before the fix.

fix_description: Added active-workspace resolution for initiatives via the app-shell workspace cookie, expanded initiative detail with real status, link/unlink, update, and delete flows, persisted initiative updates in the database-backed settings payload, fixed linked-project routing to use slugs, added the `N then I` shortcut plus targeted regression tests, and re-verified the feature against live Postgres data.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; all 637 unit tests passed after the triage fixes.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; Playwright auth setup plus smoke both passed on `http://localhost:3015`.
Logged in through the real Google OAuth flow in Ever and opened `/team/ONB/triage` under the `Onboarding QA Team` workspace.

Compared the live triage UI against `ralph/screenshots/inspect/triage.jpg`, and captured before/after evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-032-attempt-1-triage-before.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-032-attempt-1-triage-after.jpg`.

Reproduced the multi-state regression against the real database by inserting a second triage workflow state (`Needs Review`) plus issue `ONB-8`, then confirmed `GET /api/teams/ONB/triage` returned only `ONB-7` before the fix even though both `ONB-7` and `ONB-8` were stored in triage-category states.

Verified the fixed triage API and UI now show issues from both triage workflow states, render relative timestamps (`8m ago`, `9m ago`), and expose in-page filter, sort, and `Create triage issue` controls on the populated queue.

Verified the real accept flow by accepting `ONB-2`; the app issued `PATCH /api/teams/ONB/triage/6c3e1ba3-5a9f-42ad-849b-e718a3f73d3c`, the issue moved to the Backlog state in Postgres, and the queue refreshed to the empty state.

Verified the real decline flow after the fix by declining `ONB-8`; the app issued `PATCH /api/teams/ONB/triage/a43cd520-1060-46ef-8235-de1ca986785f`, the issue moved to the Canceled state in Postgres, and the queue refreshed to show only the remaining triage issue.
Ran `make check` and `make test` after the fixes; both passed (`TypeCheck passed`, `Lint & Format passed`, `637` unit tests passed).

bugs_found.description: The triage API only returned issues from the first triage workflow state, so additional triage-category states were invisible in the queue.
bugs_found.expected: The triage queue should include every issue whose workflow state category is `triage`, even when a team has multiple triage states such as `Triage` and `Needs Review`.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, `GET /api/teams/ONB/triage` matched only the first triage state id and omitted `ONB-8` even though the database stored both `ONB-7` and `ONB-8` in triage-category states.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Insert a second triage workflow state for team `ONB` and assign issue `ONB-8` to it.
2. Keep `ONB-7` in the default `Triage` state.
3. Call `GET /api/teams/ONB/triage` before the fix.
4. Observe that only `ONB-7` is returned even though both issues are in triage-category states.

bugs_found.description: The populated triage view was missing the in-page `Create triage issue`, filter, and sort controls required by the feature spec and visible in the Linear reference layout.
bugs_found.expected: A populated triage queue should still show a `Create triage issue` action plus filter and sort controls so users can manage incoming work without emptying the list first.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the populated queue only rendered rows and a footer count; the create action existed only in the empty state, and there were no filter or sort controls in the page chrome.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Ensure `/team/ONB/triage` contains at least one triage issue.
2. Open the page before the fix.
3. Observe that the populated queue has no in-page create/filter/sort controls.

bugs_found.description: Triage rows rendered calendar dates instead of the required relative timestamps.
bugs_found.expected: Each triage row should show a relative timestamp such as `13d ago`, matching the PRD and the reference screenshot.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, rows rendered absolute month/day text like `Apr 7` and `Apr 8`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/team/ONB/triage` before the fix.
2. Inspect any triage row timestamp.
3. Observe the row shows `Apr <day>` instead of a relative value such as `9m ago`.

fix_description: Updated the triage API to include all triage-category workflow states and scoped label loading to the returned issues, switched row timestamps to relative formatting, added regression coverage for multi-state triage and populated-queue controls, and rebuilt the triage page to expose create/filter/sort controls while keeping accept/decline refresh behavior wired to the real API.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; the full Vitest suite passed after the command palette fixes (`645` unit tests passed).
Ran smoke Playwright and then the required full shared-code regression suite with `make test-e2e`; the final full E2E run passed (`6 passed`).
Ran `make check`; typecheck plus Biome passed after the palette, shell, and search-route updates.
Verified the sidebar search button opens the palette in Ever on `http://localhost:3015/inbox`, and confirmed focus lands in the `Type a command or search...` input.

Verified the expanded PRD command surface in Ever: Views, Issues, Projects, Documents, Filter, Templates, and Navigation groups all render together with the footer hints `Open`, `Advanced search`, `More actions`, and `Quick look`.

Verified the real issue search path in Ever on `/team/ONB/all`: typing `ONB` now shows `Quick results for "ONB"` with live issues including `ONB-4 QA feature 004 browser verification` instead of the previous empty state.
Verified focus restoration in Ever: closing the palette with `Escape` after opening from the sidebar returns focus to the sidebar `Search` button.
Verified the custom create-issue command wiring in Ever: searching `new issue` and pressing `Enter` opens the real `Create issue for Onboarding QA Team` modal.

Saved QA evidence screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-040-attempt-1-final-inbox.jpg`, `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-040-attempt-1-search-results.jpg`, and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-040-attempt-1-quick-results.jpg`.

bugs_found.description: Live command-palette issue search was broken because the API route used an invalid `ANY(...)` filter and returned `500` for real queries.
bugs_found.expected: Typing a query such as `ONB` in the palette should return matching issues from the current workspace and show them under `Quick results for {query}`.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, `GET /api/issues/search?q=ONB` failed with Postgres error `42809: op ANY/ALL (array) requires array on right side`, so the palette fell back to an empty state instead of showing `ONB-*` issues.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/team/ONB/all`.
2. Open the command palette and type `ONB`.
3. Observe the empty state in the palette.
4. Check the dev server log and see the `ANY/ALL` Postgres error from `/api/issues/search`.

bugs_found.description: The palette did not meet the feature spec: multiple required command groups and footer hints were missing, the sidebar trigger relied on a toggle shortcut instead of an explicit open event, and focus was not restored on close.
bugs_found.expected: The palette should expose the grouped commands and footer hints described in the PRD, open reliably from the sidebar search trigger, and restore focus to the trigger after closing.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the palette showed a much smaller command list, lacked the `Advanced search`/`More actions`/`Quick look` footer hints, and closing it from the sidebar left focus on `BODY`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open the palette from the sidebar search button.
2. Compare the rendered groups and footer against the PRD.
3. Press `Escape`.
4. Observe that required groups/hints are missing and focus does not return to the search trigger.

bugs_found.description: On users with multiple workspace memberships, palette search could scope to the wrong workspace and show false empty results.
bugs_found.expected: Palette search should use the active app-shell workspace, not an arbitrary first membership row, when resolving searchable issues.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the search route just selected the first membership for the signed-in user, so multi-workspace accounts could query the wrong workspace and see no results for issues visible in the current shell.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in as a user who belongs to multiple workspaces.
2. Open the palette from one workspace shell.
3. Search for an issue identifier that exists in that active workspace.
4. Observe that the route can return no results because it scopes against another membership's workspace.

fix_description: Fixed the real search route (`inArray` plus explicit active-workspace scoping), expanded the palette to cover the required grouped commands/footer hints, hardened async search against stale responses, restored focus on close, wired the sidebar to an explicit open event, persisted the last issue for navigation, and verified the real create-issue command path plus live search results in Ever.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; the full Vitest suite passed after the custom-view fixes (`643` unit tests passed).
Ran smoke Playwright earlier in the loop and reran the required full shared-code regression suite with `make test-e2e`; the final full E2E run passed (`6 passed`).
Ran `make check`; typecheck plus Biome passed on the final code.

Opened `http://localhost:3015/views/issues` in Ever, verified the route now renders instead of 404ing, and confirmed the team sidebar `Views` link now resolves to `/views/issues?team=ONB` instead of the broken `/team/ONB/views` path.

Created an issue view in Ever for `Onboarding QA Team`, confirmed the modal now loads teams from the active workspace, saved `QA Filtered Issues View`, and verified the view list renders Name/Owner columns plus personal view actions.

Opened the saved issue view and verified it restored the real persisted filter state on `/team/ONB/all`: the page showed `Status is In Progress` and `Priority is High`, and the filtered list rendered the real `ONB-4 QA feature 004 browser verification` issue.

Edited the saved issue view name in Ever to `QA Renamed Issues View`, then deleted it successfully through the real UI after forcing `window.confirm = () => true` for browser automation compatibility.

Created a workspace-scoped project view in Ever with sort order `Progress`, opened it, and verified it navigated to `/projects` with the saved sort restored and real project data visible (`QA Empty State Project`).
Saved QA evidence screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-041-issue-view.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-041-project-view.jpg`.

bugs_found.description: The saved-views feature was not actually navigable from its expected route surface.
bugs_found.expected: Workspace views should open from `/views/issues` and `/views/projects`, and team sidebar `Views` links should land on a working custom-views page.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, `/views/issues` returned a real 404 after login, the feature only existed at `/views`, and team sidebar links pointed to `/team/{key}/views`, which was not implemented.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in and open `http://localhost:3015/views/issues` before the fix.
2. Observe the Next.js 404 page.
3. From `/team/ONB/all`, click the sidebar `Views` link under `Onboarding QA Team`.
4. Observe the app routes to an unimplemented `/team/ONB/views` path.

bugs_found.description: Saved views did not behave like saved views: they could not restore filters, open real lists, or be edited/deleted.
bugs_found.expected: Creating a custom view should persist its issue/project configuration, the row should open the corresponding filtered issue/project list, and users should be able to edit or delete saved views.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the page only supported listing and POST creation by name; rows were non-navigable, issue filters were dropped on create, project sort/filter state was not stored, and there were no edit/delete handlers or API routes.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/views` before the fix and create a view.
2. Observe that the request body posts only a name/layout shell and saves no useful filter state.
3. Try to click the resulting row or edit/delete it.
4. Observe there is no navigation behavior, no edit UI, and no delete path.

bugs_found.description: The views and projects APIs could scope to the wrong workspace for multi-workspace users, so the modal showed the wrong teams and the saved-view data could mismatch the current shell.
bugs_found.expected: Views and project data should resolve against the active workspace shown by the app shell, matching the `activeWorkspaceId` cookie behavior used by the main layout.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the APIs selected the newest membership row instead of the active workspace, so the custom-view modal surfaced teams from another workspace (`My Issues Cross-Team`, `QA Fix 20260407 1644`) while the shell was showing `Onboarding QA Team`.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in as a user who belongs to multiple workspaces.
2. Open the `Onboarding QA Team` shell and navigate to `/views/issues` before the fix.
3. Open `Create view`.
4. Observe that the team dropdown is populated from another workspace instead of the current shell.

fix_description: Refactored views into route-aware `/views/issues` and `/views/projects` pages, added saved issue/project view metadata plus edit/delete APIs, restored issue filters and project sort/filter state when opening views, corrected the team sidebar links, and fixed workspace resolution so the views and projects APIs honor the same active-workspace cookie logic as the app shell.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; the full Vitest suite passed before and after the settings fixes, finishing at `646` passing tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the smoke suite passed once Playwright could control the configured web server lifecycle.
Compared the clone's settings sidebar against `https://linear.app/settings/account/preferences` in Ever and confirmed the original includes a `Your teams` section beneath `Administration`.

Verified the pre-fix clone in Ever and Playwright: the settings shell rendered without any `Your teams` section even though the logged-in workspace had multiple teams, and saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-001-desktop-pre.png` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-001-mobile-pre.png`.
Verified the pre-fix mobile layout at `390x844`: the app shell sidebar and settings sidebar rendered side by side, leaving the settings content squeezed into a narrow strip and partially off-screen.

After the fix, verified the desktop settings shell now renders `Your teams` with real workspace teams (`Mobile`, `Platform`, `Design`, `Support`, `Operations`, `Growth`, `Onboarding QA Team`) and saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-001-desktop-post.png`.
Clicked the `Mobile` entry in the new `Your teams` section in Ever and confirmed the real team settings hub loaded in the content pane with General, Workflow, AI, and Danger zone sections.

Verified the post-fix mobile layout at `390x844`: the outer app sidebar is hidden on settings routes, the settings navigation and content stack vertically at full width, and the settings sidebar remains scrollable (`scrollHeight` greater than `clientHeight` with `overflowY: auto`). Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-001-mobile-post.png`.
Ran `make check`, `make test`, and the required full shared-layout regression suite `make test-e2e`; all passed (`6` Playwright E2E tests passed).

bugs_found.description: The settings shell omitted the required dynamic `Your teams` navigation section.
bugs_found.expected: The settings sidebar should include a `Your teams` section populated from the signed-in workspace's teams, matching the PRD and the original product's settings layout.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the clone stopped at `Administration` and rendered no team settings links at all, even though the workspace had multiple teams available in the app shell.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in to a workspace with multiple teams.
2. Open `/settings/account/preferences`.
3. Inspect the settings sidebar.
4. Observe that the sidebar ends at `Administration` and does not show a `Your teams` section or any team settings links.

bugs_found.description: The settings layout was unusable on narrow screens because the app shell sidebar and settings sidebar rendered simultaneously.
bugs_found.expected: On small screens, the settings route should remain readable and navigable without two fixed sidebars competing for the same viewport width.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the `244px` app shell sidebar and `220px` settings sidebar both stayed visible on `390px` viewports, leaving the content pane squeezed and partially off-screen.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in and open `/settings/account/preferences`.
2. Set the viewport to a mobile width such as `390x844`.
3. Observe the app shell sidebar and settings sidebar rendered side by side.
4. Note that the settings content shrinks into a narrow strip instead of using the full viewport.

fix_description: Added app-shell context so the settings layout can render workspace team links, appended the dynamic `Your teams` section with nested-route active states, hid the outer app sidebar on mobile settings routes, and stacked the settings sidebar/content layout for narrow screens while preserving desktop behavior.

tested_steps: Ran `make test`; the full Vitest suite passed after the preferences fixes (`652` unit tests passed).
Ran `make check`; typecheck plus Biome passed on the final code.
Ran `make db-push`; Drizzle applied the new `user.settings` schema change successfully to the local Postgres database.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts`; the smoke suite passed (`2` Playwright tests including auth setup).
Ran the required shared-code regression suite with `make test-e2e`; the full Playwright suite passed (`6` tests passed).

Verified the pre-fix clone in Ever at `/settings/account/preferences`: the page had only client-local state, `/api/account/preferences` returned `404`, and the required `Customize` / `Configure coding tools` controls were missing. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-002-before-fix.jpg`.

Compared the clone against the original Linear settings page in Ever and confirmed the expected behaviors for the account preferences surface: a sidebar customization control that opens a dialog, a coding tools link target, and persisted preference values.

After the fix, verified in Ever that changing Default home view, Display names, First day of week, Convert emoticons, Send comment shortcut, Font size, pointer cursors, theme, desktop-app toggle, and sidebar badge/visibility settings all save through the real authenticated `/api/account/preferences` route and survive navigation back to the page.

Verified the live UI effects from the saved preferences: theme changed to dark immediately, `fontSize` and `pointerCursors` updated `document.documentElement` datasets, the sidebar customize dialog opened with badge/visibility controls, hiding workspace Projects removed the link from the sidebar, and badge style `dot` rendered a dot unread indicator.
Verified `Default home view` now affects real navigation by saving `Inbox`, navigating to `/`, and confirming the app redirected to the inbox route instead of the generic team page.
Verified the new coding tools link navigates to the `Agent personalization` page and saved post-fix evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-002-after-fix.jpg`.

bugs_found.description: Account preferences were not persisted anywhere real.
bugs_found.expected: Changing preferences should save through an authenticated API and persist in the database so the values survive reloads and navigation.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the page held almost all values in local React state, only theme touched localStorage, and `/api/account/preferences` did not exist.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/settings/account/preferences`.
2. Change any setting except theme.
3. Reload or navigate away and back.
4. Observe that the values revert to defaults.
5. Request `/api/account/preferences` while signed in and observe there is no real route.

bugs_found.description: The App sidebar customize control required by the spec and original product was missing and non-functional.
bugs_found.expected: The Interface section should expose a `Customize` action that opens a sidebar customization surface.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the page rendered no App sidebar row or customization dialog at all.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/settings/account/preferences` before the fix.
2. Inspect the Interface section.
3. Observe that there is no `App sidebar` row and no `Customize` action.

bugs_found.description: The Coding tools link required by the feature was missing.
bugs_found.expected: The Preferences page should include a coding tools link that navigates to a live destination.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, there was no Coding tools section or navigation target from the preferences page.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Open `/settings/account/preferences` before the fix.
2. Scroll through the page.
3. Observe that no `Coding tools` section or `Configure coding tools` link is present.

bugs_found.description: Default home view was an inert selector with no effect on the app.
bugs_found.expected: The saved home-view preference should influence where `/` sends the signed-in user.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, `/` always redirected to the first team issues page regardless of the selected home view.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in and open `/settings/account/preferences` before the fix.
2. Change `Default home view`.
3. Navigate to `/`.
4. Observe that the app still redirects to the default team issues route.

fix_description: Added a real authenticated account-preferences API backed by `user.settings`, wired the preferences page to load/save through that API, applied theme/font/pointer settings live, made `Default home view` drive `/` redirects, added the sidebar customization dialog plus sidebar visibility/badge persistence, and added the coding tools destination page and navigation.

tested_steps: Ran `make test` before edits; the full Vitest suite passed (`652` unit tests passed).
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` before edits; the smoke suite passed (`2` Playwright tests including auth setup).
Verified the pre-fix profile page in Ever at `/settings/account/profile` and saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-003-attempt-1-pre-fix.jpg`.
Confirmed session-backed data loading on the live page: email and full name loaded from the authenticated Better Auth session, but username was blank because it was never persisted anywhere real.
Confirmed the pre-fix upload control was inert by clicking `Upload photo` and verifying there was no file input or upload flow in the DOM.
Confirmed the pre-fix leave-workspace action was inert by clicking `Leave workspace` and verifying no dialog opened and no navigation or membership change happened.
After the fix, uploaded a real image through the profile page and verified the authenticated `user.image` field persisted in Postgres as a data URL.
After the fix, updated the live profile to `Jaeyun Ha` / `jaeyunha`, then queried Postgres and confirmed both `user.name` and `user.settings.accountProfile.username` persisted.

Started a fresh Ever session on `/settings/account/profile` after the save and verified the page reloaded with the persisted name, username, and avatar preview. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/settings-003-attempt-1-profile-fixed.jpg`.
Opened the real leave-workspace confirmation dialog in Ever, confirmed the destructive copy referenced the active workspace, then completed the action.
Queried Postgres after confirming the leave action and verified the active `Onboarding QA Team` membership row was deleted for the signed-in user.
Verified the browser redirected to `/my-issues/assigned` after leaving, and confirmed the `activeWorkspaceId` cookie was updated to the next available workspace instead of the removed one.
Ran `make check && make test` after the fixes; typecheck, Biome, and the full Vitest suite all passed (`658` unit tests passed).
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` after the fixes; the smoke suite passed again (`2` Playwright tests including auth setup).

bugs_found.description: Username edits were not persisted anywhere real.
bugs_found.expected: The profile page should load and save the username through a real authenticated backend path so the value survives reloads and new sessions.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the page posted only to Better Auth's generic update endpoint, which updated `name` but ignored `username`, so the field always came back blank on reload.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in and open `/settings/account/profile`.
2. Enter any username and click `Update`.
3. Reload the page or start a fresh browser session.
4. Observe that the username field is empty again.

bugs_found.description: Profile-picture upload was a dead UI control.
bugs_found.expected: The profile picture section should expose a working upload path that lets the user choose an image and persist it as their avatar.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, `Upload photo` was only a styled button with no file input, no preview state, and no backend persistence path.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in and open `/settings/account/profile`.
2. Click `Upload photo`.
3. Inspect the page state.
4. Observe that no file picker, preview, or saved avatar change occurs.

bugs_found.description: Leave workspace was an inert danger action.
bugs_found.expected: The page should open a confirmation dialog and remove the user from the active workspace when the action is confirmed.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, clicking `Leave workspace` did nothing: no dialog opened, no API call ran, and the membership remained intact.
bugs_found.reproduction:
1. Sign in and open `/settings/account/profile`.
2. Click `Leave workspace`.
3. Observe that no confirmation dialog appears and the user remains in the workspace.

fix_description: Added a real authenticated account-profile API backed by `user.settings`, persisted username and avatar state through that API, wired the profile page to load/save through it, exposed a real file input with preview-backed avatar upload, and implemented a confirmed leave-workspace flow that removes the active membership, updates the active-workspace cookie, and redirects safely.
